title: Adoption risks & next product-quality improvement for pilot expansion
summary: Adoption is improving at the top of funnel (quickstart completion up to 58%), but expansion is gated by a brittle setup/credentials experience and lack of visible recovery paths. The highest-leverage next improvement is to ship a public, end-to-end setup recovery path (focused on environment/CLI/credentials) that can be demonstrated without support intervention; this is also explicitly listed as a pilot gate alongside citation transparency.

claims.text: Quickstart completion improved from 44% to 58% after the docs copy refresh, with fewer exits at the first API call step.
The largest remaining onboarding drop is during local setup and credential configuration, driven by environment-specific errors and limited recovery paths.

Support volume is dominated by environment drift, stale CLI versions, missing environment variables, and credentials copied from the wrong workspace; users often need an internal checklist to recover.
Failures often persist because users restart the quickstart and reproduce the same broken state; tickets reopen when recovery steps aren’t clear/public.
Pilot expansion is available (two teams next month) but is blocked by the most visible demo risk: setup recovery that looks brittle without documented guidance.

openQuestions: Which specific setup/credential errors account for the largest share of bounces (top 3) so the recovery checklist can be prioritized accordingly?
What minimum “recovery successful” telemetry should be added to confirm users can self-heal without restarting the quickstart?
What is the smallest lab-visible UI/UX change that proves ‘recovery guidance is hardened’ to observers (e.g., a single “Repair setup” flow vs. docs-only checklist)?
recommendation: Next product-quality improvement for pilot expansion: implement and publish a first-class setup recovery path that covers (1) stale CLI detection + upgrade steps, (2) environment variable validation, (3) credential/workspace mismatch detection + reset guidance, and (4) safe re-run behavior after partial setup—so recovery can be demonstrated without support pasting an internal checklist. Treat this as the gating release for adding the two next pilot teams.
